events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess hot work commercial project, observed field condition: permit scope exceeded. Work rough-complete. References NFPA 51B, OSHA 1926 Subpart J. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Since permit scope exceeded is non-compliant and potentially dangerous, decision fail. Risk high or critical? Hot work permit scope exceeded can be critical due to fire/explosion hazards. But maybe high. Need component_conditions: installed-defective? non-compliant? Scenario says permit scope exceeded, work roughed in. Component condition categories that may apply: installed-defective, non-compliant. The work itself is non-compliant. Could include both? installed-defective maybe not exactly; condition is non-compliant. Use ["non-compliant"] perhaps. But prompt says categories that may apply: installed-defective, non-compliant. Since work roughed in and permit scope exceeded, it's non-compliant. installed-defective maybe because installed beyond permit scope = defective installation? Could include both. Need percent_complete rough-complete maybe 75. value 0. confidence 0.95. findings: hot work performed outside authorized permit scope; uninspected/unauthorized area; lack of fire watch coverage etc. actions: stop work in affected area; reissue/revise hot work permit; inspect completed work; remove unauthorized work if required; implement fire watch; retrain. references: NFPA 51B Chapter 5 permit requirements; OSHA 1926.352? Actually Subpart J welding/cutting. Hot work permits OSHA 1926 maybe 1926.352(a) fire watchers? NFPA 51B 2019 Section 5.2 permit authorization. Need JSON only. Ensure valid JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 75,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Hot work was performed outside the authorized permit scope.",
    "Work in the affected area has not been authorized or inspected for the observed conditions.",
    "Fire prevention and watch requirements may not cover the actual work location or operations.",
    "Completed hot work in the unauthorized scope cannot be accepted as compliant."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop hot work immediately in the area where the permit scope was exceeded.",
    "Revise or reissue the hot work permit to accurately reflect the actual scope, location, and hazards.",
    "Inspect the completed work and adjacent areas for fire damage, slag, or ignition sources.",
    "Remove or rework unauthorized hot work if it cannot be brought within an approved permit scope.",
    "Provide retraining on hot work permit limits and NFPA/OSHA requirements before resuming.",
    "Ensure a qualified fire watch is assigned for the full authorized scope before work restarts."
  ],
  "rationale": "NFPA 51B and OSHA 1926 Subpart J require hot work to be performed only within the limits of an authorized hot work permit. Exceeding the permit scope means the work was not properly evaluated for fire hazards, fire watch coverage, and safe conditions, making it non-compliant and unsafe to accept.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 51B, Standard for Fire Prevention During Welding, Cutting, and Other Hot Work, permit authorization and scope requirements",
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart J, Welding and Cutting, fire prevention and protection requirements"
  ]
}
